hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions in the ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 region while reducing off-target affinity. This will increase the selectivity margin by improving SSTR2 complementarity and disrupting off-target electrostatic/steric compatibility.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is generating strong SSTR2 binders but insufficiently addressing off-target interactions. A focused mutation strategy at non-pharmacophore positions is necessary to improve selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are known to contribute to off-target binding (particularly with SSTR3/SSTR5). Focusing mutations here can disrupt conserved pocket contacts while preserving the pharmacophore.
These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved hydrophobic or electrostatic interactions with off-target SSTRs while preserving flexibility and compatibility with the pharmacophore.
